mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, labor market deterioration signals severe corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output capacity, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts expectations of tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth surpasses 2.0% annually, aggregate demand expansion supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total employment and rising unemployment signals structural labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence metric shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates during a liquidity trap eliminate short-term borrowing costs, forcing investors into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
